have such faith in Roosevelt's

Abraham Lincoln by stroke OF
his pen freed four million slaves
an dthis one act made his name
immortal. Teddy's fame is already
international, and his popularity
among American voters is so great
that his efforts on behalf of the
political emancipation of more thar
one half of the nations inhabitants
would entitle him to a niche in the
hall OF fame, and the mothers would
rise and call him blessed because
he helped to make them free.


We need a man in the state OF
Washington noted as is Mr.. Roose
welt, for the courage of his convic
tons. The resolution committee
of the Spokane county republican
convention needed the leavenine
force of his stern personality to in
duce them to stand by the action
of the state legislature, overwhelm
ingly republican, who submitted
thte amendment to the voters oil
the state granting the right of sui
frage to women.


If the great state of Washington
needed any other recommendation
than its natural resources warrant
Teddy Roosevelt's activities in the
woman's suffrage campaign now
being waged would certainly sup
ply the need.


Think of it-Washington the Ike!
that would make woman's suffrage
a national issue! May Arkwright
Hutton.
